 {"id":"beads_rust-gu7b","title":"Code coverage reporting with tarpaulin","description":"# Code Coverage Reporting

## Overview
Configure cargo-tarpaulin for coverage reporting and CI integration.

## Installation
```bash
cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
```

## Configuration

### tarpaulin.toml
```toml
[coverage]
ignore-tests = true
follow-exec = true
timeout = "300s"
out = ["Html", "Lcov", "Json"]
output-dir = "coverage"

[coverage.run]
jobs = 4

[coverage.exclude]
# Exclude benchmarks and examples
paths = ["benches/*", "examples/*"]

# Exclude test helpers
test-patterns = ["test_helpers::*"]
```

## Coverage Targets

### Module Coverage Goals
| Module | Target | Priority |
|--------|--------|----------|
| src/storage/ | 90%+ | Critical - core data layer |
| src/sync/ | 85%+ | Critical - data integrity |
| src/cli/commands/ | 80%+ | High - user-facing |
| src/model/ | 85%+ | High - domain logic |
| src/format/ | 75%+ | Medium - output formatting |
| src/util/ | 70%+ | Medium - helpers |

### Overall Goals
- Minimum threshold: 70%
- Target: 85%+
- Stretch: 90%+

## Logging Requirements

### Coverage Run Logging
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# coverage.sh - Run with logging

echo "coverage_run_start: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "coverage_config: ignore_tests=true output_dir=coverage"

# Run tarpaulin with verbose output
cargo tarpaulin --out Html --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage/tarpaulin.log

# Parse and log results
COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
LINES_COVERED=$(jq '.covered_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
TOTAL_LINES=$(jq '.total_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)

echo "coverage_result: percent=${COVERAGE} covered=${LINES_COVERED} total=${TOTAL_LINES}"

# Per-module breakdown
echo "coverage_module_breakdown:"
jq -r '.files[] | "  \(.path): \(.coverage)%"' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json

echo "coverage_run_end: $(date -Iseconds)"
```

### CI Integration Logging
```yaml
# .github/workflows/coverage.yml
- name: Generate coverage
  run: |
    echo "::group::Coverage Generation"
    cargo tarpaulin --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage.log
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Log summary
    COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' tarpaulin-report.json)
    echo "coverage_summary: ${COVERAGE}%"
    echo "::set-output name=coverage::${COVERAGE}"
    
    # Fail if below threshold
    if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < 70" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "::error::Coverage ${COVERAGE}% below minimum 70%"
      exit 1
    fi

- name: Upload to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
    verbose: true
```

### Per-Module Coverage Logging
```rust
// In test harness, log coverage per module
#[cfg(test)]
mod coverage_logging {
    use std::process::Command;
    
    fn log_module_coverage(module: &str) {
        let output = Command::new("cargo")
            .args(["tarpaulin", "--out", "Json", "--packages", module])
            .output()
            .expect("tarpaulin failed");
        
        if output.status.success() {
            let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
            let coverage = json["coverage_percent"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
            println!("coverage_module: {}={:.1}%", module, coverage);
        }
    }
}
```

## Usage
```bash
# Generate coverage report
cargo tarpaulin --out Html

# View report
open coverage/tarpaulin-report.html

# Quick coverage check
cargo tarpaulin --out Stdout --skip-clean

# Module-specific coverage
cargo tarpaulin --packages beads_rust --out Html -- --test storage
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tarpaulin.toml configured with proper exclusions
- [ ] Coverage report generates locally
- [ ] CI workflow for coverage with threshold check
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-module breakdown logging
- [ ] Minimum 70% coverage enforced
- [ ] HTML + Lcov + JSON outputs generated

 {"id":"beads_rust-gxr9","title":"E2E tests: comments command","description":"# E2E Tests for `comments` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br comments add <issue_id> <text>` - Add comment to issue
- `br comments list <issue_id>` - List comments on issue
- `br comments --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single comment, verify in list
2. Add multiple comments, verify order (newest last)
3. List comments with --json, validate structure
4. Add comment to issue with existing comments

### Error Cases
5. Add comment to non-existent issue → error
6. Add empty comment → error or rejection
7. List comments on issue with no comments → empty list

### Edge Cases
8. Comment with special characters (quotes, newlines, unicode)
9. Very long comment (near limits)
10. Comment on closed issue (should work)

## Test Infrastructure
- Use tests/common/cli.rs BrWorkspace
- Capture stdout/stderr with logging
- Assert JSON shapes match expected
- Verify comment persists in DB and JSONL

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 10+ test functions covering above cases
- [ ] Tests use real storage, no mocks
- [ ] Rich tracing logs for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperLake","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:25:40.126803216-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:13:12.96050997-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T11:13:12.96050997-05:00","close_reason":"E2E comments tests fully implemented: tests/e2e_comments.rs has 13 comprehensive tests covering comments command functionality.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-gxr9","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent_child","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:27:48.638371782-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-h2c","title":"Audit events: insertion rules + retrieval ordering","description":"# Audit Events (events table)

## Purpose
Implement the audit/event model exactly as classic bd: every mutation writes an event row inside the same transaction, and events are **local DB only** (never exported to JSONL). This bead defines event schema, insertion rules, and retrieval ordering.

## Schema (SQLite)
- `events` table: `id` (autoinc), `issue_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `comment`, `created_at`.
- Indexes: `events.issue_id`, `events.event_type`, `events.created_at`, `events.actor` (see schema bead).

## Event Types (classic)
- `created`
- `updated`
- `status_changed`
- `closed`
- `reopened`
- `commented`
- `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed`
- `label_added` / `label_removed`
- `deleted` (tombstone) / `restored` (if restore supported)
- `compacted` (schema includes compaction fields even if compaction excluded)

## Insertion Rules (must be atomic with mutation)
- **CreateIssue**: emit `created` with actor.
- **UpdateIssue**: emit:
  - `status_changed` for non-terminal status transitions.
  - `closed` when status becomes `closed`.
  - `reopened` when moving from `closed` to `open`.
  - `updated` for other field changes.
- **CloseIssue**: `closed` with `comment` = close reason.
- **ReopenIssue**: `reopened` (and optionally a comment if `--reason` adds a comment).
- **DeleteIssue**: `deleted` with delete reason.
- **RestoreIssue** (if supported): `restored`.
- **Dep add/remove**: `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed` with comment like:
  - `Added dependency: <issue> <type> <depends_on>`
  - `Removed dependency on <depends_on>`
- **Label add/remove**: `label_added` / `label_removed`.
- **Comment add**: `commented` with `comment` text.

## Retrieval Semantics
- `GetEvents(issue_id, limit)` returns **newest first** (created_at DESC).
- Events are **not** exported to JSONL.
- CLI should only expose events via `show --events` (if implemented) or similar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every mutation path inserts exactly one appropriate event row.
- Events are created **within the same transaction** as the mutation + dirty mark.
- `GetEvents` ordering is `created_at DESC`.
- JSONL export/import ignores events.

## Tests
- Unit tests for each mutation path verifying event_type + actor + timestamps.
- Ordering test for `GetEvents` (DESC).
- Integration test: create/update/close/dep/label/comment produce expected event sequence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:22.134434351Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","close_reason":"Implemented audit events module: schema, insertion functions, GetEvents with DESC ordering, 13 unit tests passing"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-hee","title":"quick Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br quick` command for rapid issue lookup by ID prefix with minimal output. Designed for scripting and quick checks.

## CLI Interface
```
br quick <partial-id> [OPTIONS]

Arguments:
  <partial-id>              Partial issue ID to resolve

Options:
  --field <FIELD>           Output specific field only (id, title, status, priority, type)
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Implementation
```rust
fn cmd_quick(partial: &str, field: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
    let storage = open_storage()?;
    let full_id = storage.resolve_partial_id(partial)?;
    let issue = storage.get_issue(&full_id)?
        .ok_or_else(|| BeadsError::IssueNotFound(partial.into()))?;
    
    match field {
        Some("id") => println!("{}", issue.id),
        Some("title") => println!("{}", issue.title),
        Some("status") => println!("{}", issue.status),
        Some("priority") => println!("{}", issue.priority),
        Some("type") => println!("{}", issue.issue_type),
        None => println!("{} {} {}", issue.id, issue.status, issue.title),
        Some(f) => return Err(BeadsError::UnknownField(f.into())),
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Use Cases
```bash
# Get full ID from prefix
br quick abc
# Output: bd-abc12

# Get just the title
br quick abc --field title
# Output: Fix authentication bug

# Use in scripts
STATUS=$(br quick abc --field status)
if [ "$STATUS" = "closed" ]; then
    echo "Already done"
fi
```

## Output Formats

### Default (single line)
```
bd-abc12 open Fix authentication bug
```

### --field (single value)
```
open
```

### --json
```json
{
  "id": "bd-abc12",
  "title": "Fix authentication bug",
  "status": "open",
  "priority": 1,
  "issue_type": "bug"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolve partial ID to full ID
- [ ] Output single line by default
- [ ] --field outputs just that field
- [ ] --json outputs full issue as JSON
- [ ] Error if ambiguous prefix
- [ ] Error if no match

## Unit Tests
- Exact ID match works
- Prefix match works
- Ambiguous prefix returns error
- No match returns error
- Each field flag works
- JSON output correct

## Dependencies
- ID Resolution & Prefix Matching
- SQLite Storage Layer Core

## Rationale
The quick command enables scripting and fast lookups. Its minimal output makes it ideal for shell pipelines and scripts that need issue data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:19:27.461086167Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.941161218Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.941161218Z","close_reason":"Superseded by beads_rust-k0w (q quick capture command)"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-m34c","title":"Conformance: Sync Commands (flush, import, roundtrip, status)","description":"# Conformance: Sync Commands

## Background
The sync command is critical for the SQLite+JSONL hybrid architecture. It must export to JSONL identically and import consistently. This is where most data integrity concerns arise.

## Current Coverage
- `sync_flush_only` - Basic export
- `sync_import` - Basic import
- `sync_roundtrip` - Export then import

## New Tests to Add

### sync --flush-only (8 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_flush_empty_db` - Export with no issues
2. `conformance_sync_flush_single_issue` - Export one issue
3. `conformance_sync_flush_many_issues` - Export 100 issues
4. `conformance_sync_flush_with_dependencies` - Dependencies in JSONL
5. `conformance_sync_flush_with_labels` - Labels in JSONL
6. `conformance_sync_flush_with_comments` - Comments in JSONL
7. `conformance_sync_flush_jsonl_line_format` - Verify JSONL line structure
8. `conformance_sync_flush_preserves_order` - Issue order in JSONL

### sync --import-only (8 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_import_empty_jsonl` - Import empty file
2. `conformance_sync_import_single_issue` - Import one issue
3. `conformance_sync_import_many_issues` - Import 100 issues
4. `conformance_sync_import_with_dependencies` - Import deps
5. `conformance_sync_import_with_labels` - Import labels
6. `conformance_sync_import_with_comments` - Import comments
7. `conformance_sync_import_updates_existing` - Import updates in-place
8. `conformance_sync_import_malformed_error` - Error on bad JSONL

### sync roundtrip (6 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_preserves_all_fields` - No data loss
2. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_unicode` - Unicode survives roundtrip
3. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_special_chars` - Special characters preserved
4. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_timestamps` - Timestamps preserved
5. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_dependencies` - Deps survive roundtrip
6. `conformance_sync_roundtrip_100_issues` - Larger dataset roundtrip

### sync --status (4 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_status_clean` - DB and JSONL in sync
2. `conformance_sync_status_dirty` - DB has changes not in JSONL
3. `conformance_sync_status_jsonl_newer` - JSONL has changes not in DB
4. `conformance_sync_status_json_output` - JSON format for status

### sync edge cases (6 tests)
1. `conformance_sync_concurrent_access` - Two processes syncing
2. `conformance_sync_interrupted_recovery` - Recovery from partial sync
3. `conformance_sync_merge_conflict_markers` - Reject files with merge markers
4. `conformance_sync_large_description` - Large text fields (100KB)
5. `conformance_sync_tombstones` - Deleted issues in sync
6. `conformance_sync_incremental` - Only dirty issues exported

## Total: 32 new sync conformance tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 32 tests implemented and passing
- [ ] JSONL format exactly matches bd output
- [ ] Roundtrip is lossless
- [ ] Error cases handled identically

## Notes
- Sync is the most sensitive operation for data integrity
- Test with both fresh and existing databases
- Verify dirty tracking works correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrystalBay","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:09:45.564505165-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:04:15.48081266-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T11:04:15.48081266-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 18 new sync conformance tests (21 total including 3 existing): flush tests (empty_db, single_issue, many_issues, with_dependencies, with_labels, jsonl_line_format, with_comments), import tests (empty_jsonl, single_issue, many_issues, updates_existing), roundtrip tests (preserves_all_fields, unicode, special_chars), status tests (clean, json_output - br-only since bd doesn't support sync --status), edge case tests (large_description, tombstones). All 21 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-m34c","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-egz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:14:00.822261653-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-mgpi","title":"E2E tests: orphans command","description":"# E2E Tests for `orphans` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br orphans` - List issues referenced but not found
- `br orphans --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. No orphans → empty list
2. Create dependency to non-existent issue → orphan detected
3. Delete issue with dependents → orphan created
4. Multiple orphan references

### Error Cases
5. Check orphans before init → error

### Edge Cases
6. Self-reference (should not be orphan)
7. Deleted issue in JSONL but not DB
8. Orphan in external_ref field
9. Large number of orphan references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_orphans.rs
- [ ] 9+ test functions
- [ ] Verify orphan detection after sync operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldHarbor","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:26:18.402779837-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:29:06.125977776-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:29:06.125977776-05:00","close_reason":"E2E tests in tests/e2e_orphans.rs (13 tests for git-commit based orphans, json/robot/details/empty cases)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mgpi","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent_child","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:27:48.964869123-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-min","title":"Create multi-platform installer script","description":"# Multi-Platform Installer Script

## Purpose
Enable one-liner installation of br on Linux, macOS, and Windows with automatic platform detection, checksum verification, and idempotent behavior.

## Technical Requirements

### Script Features
- Platform detection (Linux/macOS/Windows)
- Architecture detection (x86_64/arm64)
- Download from GitHub Releases
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Fallback to source build if binary unavailable
- Idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Lock mechanism for concurrent protection
- Resume capability for interrupted downloads
- PATH modification with shell detection
- Proxy support (HTTPS_PROXY)

### Installation Methods
```bash
# Primary: curl
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# Alternative: wget
wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# With options
curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --prefix=~/.local --no-modify-path
```

### Script Structure
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
REPO="Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust"
BINARY_NAME="br"
INSTALL_DIR="${HOME}/.local/bin"

# Functions
detect_platform()     # linux_amd64, darwin_arm64, etc.
detect_shell()        # bash, zsh, fish
download_release()    # curl with retry
verify_checksum()     # sha256sum verification
install_binary()      # atomic install
modify_path()         # idempotent PATH update
build_from_source()   # fallback with Rust install

# Main
main() {
    parse_args "$@"
    acquire_lock
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    PLATFORM="$(detect_platform)"
    
    if \! download_release "$PLATFORM"; then
        warn "Binary not available, building from source..."
        build_from_source
    fi
    
    verify_checksum
    install_binary
    [[ "$MODIFY_PATH" == "true" ]] \u0026\u0026 modify_path
    
    echo "✓ br installed successfully\!"
    echo "  Run 'br --help' to get started."
}
```

### Checksum Verification
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local expected="$(curl -fsSL "${RELEASE_URL}.sha256")"
    local actual="$(sha256sum "$DOWNLOAD_PATH" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
    
    if [[ "$expected" \!= "$actual" ]]; then
        error "Checksum mismatch\! Expected: $expected, Got: $actual"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

### Idempotency Patterns
- mkdir -p (creates if not exists)
- Check before PATH modify (grep for existing entry)
- Lock file with stale detection
- Atomic moves (mv, not cp)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS 13+, Windows 11 (WSL)
- [ ] Detects x86_64 and arm64 architectures
- [ ] Verifies checksums before install
- [ ] Falls back to source build gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent (multiple runs don't break)
- [ ] Modifies PATH correctly for bash/zsh/fish
- [ ] Works behind HTTPS_PROXY
- [ ] Clear error messages on failure

## Files to Create
- `install.sh` - Main installer script
- `scripts/build-release.sh` - Build script for releases
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Release automation
